moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Reduce SPY to 20% to meet concentration mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI and QQQ to favor income and broad index exposure.
Buy META at 10% as a top-quality name with a strong scanner setup.
Maintain a cash buffer for capital preservation.
This plan aims for steady, low-drawdown performance.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets approximately 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and diversifying holdings. Expect lower volatility and smaller drawdowns, but also more limited upside potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure to zero.
Increase QQQI and QQQ to balance income and broad index.
Buy XLK at 10% and add to SMH at 15% as quality tech with strong setups.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer.
This plan blends growth with risk management.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets approximately 2.5% per month with a focus on risk-first growth. Leverage is removed, and concentration is reduced to manage risk. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns, with a blend of steady growth and quality tech exposure.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration mandate.
Add to SOXL up to 10% to use leveraged ETFs for growth.
Increase SMH to 20% and CRWD to 15% to press what is working and concentrate.
Buy META at 15% and XLK at 10% as high-scoring scanner setups.
Maintain a small cash buffer.
This plan stretches for growth using leverage and concentration.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets approximately 4% per month, stretching for growth with increased leverage and concentration. This approach raises both upside and drawdown potential significantly. Be aware that leveraged ETFs decay daily, and this strategy can lose a lot fast if strict stops are not adhered to.

moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration mandate.
Sell all SOXL, SPAL, and CRWD to eliminate leveraged ETFs and high-beta single names.
Reduce SMH to 5% to further reduce high-beta exposure.
Increase QQQI to 40% and QQQ to 20% to prioritize income and broad index exposure.
Maintain a cash buffer.
This plan focuses on steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
income.read: This income plan targets approximately 1% per month by rotating into income and broad index ETFs. The priority is low drawdown and reliable income, but distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff. This is cash-like income, not a savings account, with reduced growth potential.
